edge_cases_considered: Distinguished the review artifact's own required correction from the underlying F01 design specification and the pre-existing SIGTERM source comment.
Verified the review artifact provenance from the introducing commit rather than relying only on the mutable working tree.
Included both the forced-exit outcome contract and the broader residue/cleanup contract lines.
evidence: docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:195-213 states the watchdog contradiction and requires a coordinator-owned watchdog, a distinct forced-exit outcome, cleanup below the hard deadline, forced-path tests, and an exact residue contract.
docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:215-240 specifies missing cleanup APIs and the complete residue fixture set.
docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md:284 and :303-304 summarize that the watchdog may bypass cleanup and require reconciliation with I2/I5/I7/A0 plus a complete residue set.
docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F01/design.md:140-178 defines the sole coordinator, phase ordering, cleanup list, and OS-thread watchdog hard bound.
docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F01/design.md:252-269 defines I2/I5/I7, including watchdog-enforced bounds and watchdog preemption as the only cleanup skip.
docs/fork/ideal-base/evidence/F01/design.md:277-281 requires runtime exit plus zero residue.
docs/fork/ideal-base/ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md:7-15 requires one bounded authority, zero descendants, and coherent-or-removed sidecars.
crates/jcode-app-core/src/server.rs:1213-1219 is the source comment and implementation stating the watchdog guarantees exit if cleanup stalls and then forces process exit.

Review artifact source: commit 7563a123709d92e0e0505d44cb1d43e0af705743, subject 'docs: add F01 architecture critique', path docs/fork/ideal-base/reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md. The artifact itself says it reviewed commit 93c59a218 at lines 3-6.
artifact.findings: Exact review lines are 195-213 for the coordinator-owned forced-exit watchdog and outcome/residue contract, and 215-240 for cleanup/residue expectations. The strongest exact wording is lines 209-213: make the watchdog owned and armed by the single coordinator, define forced-exit separately from successful Exited, budget drain plus cleanup below the deadline, and test the forced path against an exact residue contract. Supporting spec/comment lines are design.md 140-178, 252-269, 277-281; ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md 7-15; and server.rs 1213-1219.
artifact.validation: Used numbered reads from the working tree and independently re-read the same ranges via git show from commit 7563a123709d92e0e0505d44cb1d43e0af705743. Used git log/blame to establish provenance. No edits were made.
what_i_did_not_check: I did not assess whether the current uncommitted F02 implementation satisfies these contracts, because the task requested line location only.
I did not inspect unrelated watchdogs such as request-handler or heap-retention watchdogs beyond excluding them as irrelevant.
